author: fab_10
content:
The report reports what is my hypothesis:
> Call timing. prysm and teku wait longer after the build trigger before calling getPayload, so assembly has finished and the call returns immediately. lighthouse, lodestar and nimbus call earlier and block on the remaining assembly work — which at 160M gas is ~300 ms and at 12M gas is ~0 ms. This is the reading the data favours: at low load the "early" callers pay nothing, exactly as you'd expect if the only cost is waiting. 
this should be verified by the time that elapses between the FcU+attrs and the getPayload response, if that duration is similar between CLs, then Besu is always taking the same amount of time building a block, and the different getPayload response time it due to the timing the CLs choose to get the payload.

author: qu0b
content: I was referring to this chart tho in my analysis

author: qu0b
content: see how the baseline is much lower before the orange point and never goes down again after the spam

author: fab_10
content: second report seems to say a different story, the only odd thing seems to be Lighthouse increased response time after the load

author: fab_10
content: while all the others CLs are back to pre-load levels

content: true
